Originally, from Chicago, now living Hong Kong, Jeanne studied improvisation Chicago.
She holds B.S.
Anthropology from Loyola University Chicago and M.S.
Historic Preservation from the School the Art Institute.
She has performed, presented and conducted workshops the US, Europe and Asia.
Ms. Lambin’s workshops have been described “amazing,” “powerful,” and “great fun.” Her work has been featured the Hong Kong’s Top Notch Storytelling Festival, Liar’s League Hong Kong, Literary Death Match, the Art’s House World Voices Presents, the Moth Chicago, Story Lab and This Much True.
 Lambin proud founding member Improv for Humanity and co-creator The Quest: Improv for Transformation.

the past five years the percentage weekly mobile banking users has more than tripled from <hide> <hide>% <hide> while weekly branch visitors fell from <hide>% <hide>% the same time frame.
<hide> marked the first time that weekly mobile bankers (<hide>%) exceeded branch bankers (<hide>%) Javelin’s survey.
“What driving this incredible growth?
Smartphones and tablets are gaining rapidly consumer adoption but it’s also function the fact that mobile banking services have not only proliferated but increased the quality and features they offer consumers,” said Javelin analyst Daniel Van Dyke during January webinar reviewing the firm’s Mobile Banking, Smartphone and Tablet Forecast.
Javelin’s recent report Digital Account Opening Reaches The Tipping Point also found that more Americans are applying for credit cards, loans and investment products through digital channels than physical ones.
